Title: Jericho Swain
Age: Current46 – 51 years old Timeline Born946 AN – 951 AN Edit

children.title: Early life
children.textContent:
Jericho Swain was born into an affluent Noxian family, among the established houses that trace shortly after the nation's founding. They were also favored by the emperor of Noxus― Boram Darkwill―having been instrumental in his rise to the throne.

Yet House Swain was also among many that hungered for power, and as such were part of a secret cabal called the Black Rose. His parents were especially intent on finding some dark power hidden in the depths of the Immortal Bastion, that which eluded discovery for many years as it cackled in the darkness like a raven's caw.

Though the cabal had aided Darkwill's political rise, they began to plot against him after he refused their counsel. Swain was informed of their conspiracy by LeBlanc, herself the secret matriarch of the organization, and he revealed the cult and executed its most prominent members, including his own parents and LeBlanc herself. For exposing this corruption, Swain was granted a commission in the Noxian army, where he would serve for years to come.

children.title: Discovery of Raum
children.textContent:
Swain learned firsthand that the empire's true strength was upheld not by pure Noxian ferocity, but by all its people united in spite of their origins. Nobles and foreign slaves alike were all seen as soldiers on the front lines. Swain carved out his career in securing the western borders, as well as expanding into Shurima. But in time, the aging emperor grew more paranoid and unhinged over his own mortality. Fighting wars on too many fronts, lusting over magical relics; greed became the clear motivation of the regime.

During the invasion of Ionia, Darkwill retasked many of the warbands to hunt for artifacts that could extend a mortal's lifespan. Swain's forces were soon depleted and it became nearly impossible to engage the enemy. However, Swain still managed to capture the Placidium of Navori and took its defenders hostage, hoping to lure in Ionian reinforcements. A young girl, Irelia, led the resistance to take back the Placidium. Using her ancient blade dancing technique, she cut down many of Swain's veterans and struck down the general himself, taking his arm.

What should have been a successful ambush, turned into a failure as his troops were eventually overrun. With his knee shattered and his arm severed, Swain was on the verge of death. He felt a familiar darkness press upon him again as a raven approached to feed, but he refused to let it take him. Staring into the bird's eyes, he saw a vision of the evil strangling the heart of Noxus: a black rose, a pale woman, and her puppet emperor. Swain realized that he had failed to defeat the cabal from his youth and that they had seduced Darkwill into betraying him. Knowing this vision came from more than just a raven, Swain suspected this was the dark power that his parents once searched for.

Returning to Noxus, Swain was discharged for his failure in Ionia and was regarded as nothing more than a cripple. Despite his fragile state, Swain ventured into the Immortal Bastion in search of the strange power, that which cackled in the darkness like a raven's caw. What he found was Raum, an ancient entity that preyed on the dying and consumed their secrets. But the man solved a puzzle that not even the demon could have anticipated: a way to wield its power.

children.title: Establishment of the Trifarix
children.textContent:
Using the demon's magic, Swain was able to heal himself. Over the course of many years, Swain began to plot his revenge against Darkwill. Among the people he allied himself with was Draven, a washed-up gladiator and the brother of the current Hand of Noxus, Darius. Swain also counted General Marcus Du Couteau, the empire's greatest assassin, an ally as well.

When the time came, Swain and his allies seized control of Noxus in a single night. He slew Darkwill using magic that had never been seen before so that all of Noxus could bear witness. As part of his coup, Swain lured Urgot to Zaun to ensure that he didn't aid the emperor.

The Black Rose attempted to stop Swain's coup by unleashing their living weapons against him, even their barely controlled homunculus Briar. Despite slaughtering everyone in her path, including her handler, Swain managed to escape. Eventually, Swain's guards were able to activate her pillory to restrain her, allowing them to detain her.

In the aftermath of Swain's coup, General Darius returned to Noxus from a long campaign in the Freljord. As the Hand of Noxus, many expected him to avenge Darkwill. However, Darius greatly respected Swain and his duty as the Hand was to serve Noxus, not any one ruler. Swain met with Darius, and he was satisfied with the coup against Darkwill and Swain's vision for the future of Noxus. Instead of seizing the throne, Swain wanted to ensure that no one ruler, not even himself, could ever corrupt Noxus. Thus, he established a triumvirate called the Trifarix to rule the empire. Each seat was held by a representative of one of the three guiding Noxian principles: Vision, embodied by Swain himself; Might, embodied by Darius; and Guile, embodied by the elusive "Faceless".

This council was made so that no one man could rule Noxus, and if one were to divert from the foundations of strength that Noxus was built on, the other two would be able to keep them in check. Moreover, this insurance would also serve as a counterbalance to Swain should he ever fall to the demon's corruption.

Swain immediately pulled back the Noxian forces from Darkwill's untenable ventures. He also held conventions with the noble houses, detailing his plans for the empire. A number of them honored his coup and he pardoned those that swore fealty, but many others did not and were allowed to leave the empire or were sent to the arena to be executed by Draven. A large part of the dissent was over the new policies that grated traditional power systems such as inherited lordship, personal wealth, and ownership of human property.

Under the Trifarix's rule, the empire changed its philosophy regarding those they conquered. Rather than crushing those weaker than them, Noxus began to assimilate the nations they conquered. They believed that their diverse talents strengthened the empire, and in turn the empire would support their reconstruction. However, there were still those who clung to the old ways and opposed Swain's vision.

Shortly after the end of the invasion of Ionia, Swain dispatched his ravens to keep an eye on General Ambessa Medarda and the Black Rose as they headed to Piltover to investigate a new invention called Hextech, and observed what ensued in the Twin Cities.

children.title: The Black Powder Plot
children.textContent: Secessionists held the mineral-rich Rokrund Plains, crafting explosives that deterred Noxian forces from advancing. This prompted Swain to visit the campaign generals and discuss strategy. One of these was General Brannin Granth, a secret operative of the Black Rose that plotted to kill Swain. Within the meeting, he proclaimed that the battle was unwinnable without death and revealed an explosive fuse ready to blow. Swain chuckled at the profound suggestion, agreeing that while the soldiers may die anyway, their sacrifice makes way for a greater reinforcement. He then reveals his demon arm and quickly strangles Granth. "Granth and I... we always look for what must be sacrificed in order to win."

children.title: The Delverhold Affair
children.textContent:
Seven years into the Trifarix rule, delegates Oram and Alyssa from the Delverhold approached the council to negotiate concessions in response to a local crisis. The mineral export quotas became unsustainable with the dwindling reserves in the Ironspike mines, sinking the people into poverty. Furthermore, Oram argued that their treaty to the empire had been invalidated with the death of the emperor, which permits the Delverhold to extricate from the empire if necessary. While Swain agreed to hear the terms, Darius argued that there was no compelling bargain.

Some days go by, and the delegates attempted to seize and extort the Faceless One for their vote. But in a deceptive trap, Oram is killed by Alyssa and she is quickly captured by the guards. She is brought before the Trifarix, whom were fully aware of the extortion, but recognize her disaffection in the Delverhold. Swain candidly stated that the Trifarix did not want to encourage other provinces to make demands as well. Alyssa said that she knew the Delverhold governor would use the delegates' deaths to justify secession, but returning Delverhold to the kingdom rule would be a grave mistake long-term. She then suggested to become governor herself in order to maintain relations with Noxus. The council agree to the idea and sent a Trifarian entourage with her back to Delverhold to claim office.

children.title: Assassinating Jarvan III
children.textContent:
Four years after the coup against Darkwill, Swain dispatched General Marcus Du Couteau to infiltrate Demacia and assassinate King Jarvan III, leading him to fake his own death. In the three years that followed, Marcus's daughter, Katarina, took up work for Swain, assassinating his political opponents, and began to earn his favor. However, Swain learned that Marcus had been compromised, and decided to replace him with Katarina as the king's assassin and marked Marcus as her secondary target. In an attempt to turn Katarina against him, Marcus intercepted his orders and revealed himself to his daughter, claiming that Swain had fallen to the demon's corruption. Meanwhile, Swain observed her through the eyes of his ravens.

Katarina remained loyal to Swain's vision for Noxus and was betrayed by her father after assassinating the king. When she returned to Noxus, Katarina sought to protect the Grand General from her father after he shrouded himself from his sight with the aid of the Black Rose. Marcus planned to detonate wolf's maw explosive within Swain's vicinity, but Katarina was able to wrench it from his grasp. Unable to disarm it, Katarina planned to hurl it, and herself, far enough away to save Noxus. Before she could act, Swain seized the explosive and destroyed it and Marcus with a single blast.

Swain apologized for arriving so late, explaining that he had been unable to sense Marcus, though he was able to see Katarina. He admitted that he was no longer the same man he once was, but argued that this was precisely why Noxus needed an assassin like Katarina, someone capable of reaching even him in case he fell to the demon's corruption. Swain thanked Katarina for her service to the empire and told her that she had earned his trust, to which Katarina assured him that she knew exactly how to kill him. In the aftermath, Swain named Katarina the new head of the Du Couteau assassins guild.

mainContent.title: Appearance
mainContent.textContent: Swain is an older, fair skinned man with long, white hair. He wears black armor, black pants, and a black coat. His left arm was restored with the power of Raum, and consequently glows a shade of red.

mainContent.title: Personality
mainContent.textContent: Swain is cold and calculating, often seeing people's lives as pieces in a bigger game. He is a master tactician and general, able to predict his enemies' next move with his cunning intellect and win battles swiftly and efficiently. Swain does not often act in his own interests, rather doing whatever is best for Noxus, as he knows that the empire is much more important than him, and he is committed to returning it back to its foundations and making sure that it outlasts him.

children.title: Irelia
children.textContent: Irelia cut off Swain's left arm during the Battle of the Placidium.

children.title: Darius
children.textContent: Swain and Darius are two of the three heads of the Trifarix of Noxus, embodying the Noxian values of Vision and Might respectively, along with The Faceless One, who embodies Guile. Prior to the establishment of the Trifarix, Darius greatly respected Swain and argued against his discharge from the army.

children.title: Draven
children.textContent: When audiences for the Reckoning arenas waned and Draven fell into a drunk stupor, Swain found him and offered him a role in his coup against Boram Darkwill. He became integral to Swain's plan to kill Darkwill. He is considered a close ally by Swain and executed the remaining the dissenting houses after the establishment of the Trifarix.

children.title: Katarina
children.textContent: Katarina serves as an assassin for the Trifarix council, killing people at their command. She earned Swain's trust after she stopped her father from assassinating him and is tasked by Swain with eliminating him should he fall to the demon's corruption.

children.title: Urgot
children.textContent: Urgot was sent away from Noxus to Zaun as a part of Swain's coup, as he was seen as a liability and a potential threat to Swain's plan.

children.title: Gangplank
children.textContent: Gangplank stole The Leviathan, Swain's personal warship and the head of the Noxian fleet. Because of this, Swain despises Gangplank and wishes to see his demise.

children.title: LeBlanc
children.textContent: Swain considers LeBlanc, the head matriarch of the Black Rose, as his rival. Swain and LeBlanc's personal relationship is vague, and they may have been lovers at some point. When Boram Darkwill first took the throne and refused help from the Black Rose, LeBlanc, unbeknownst to Swain, manipulated Swain into revealing the Black Rose and making it look like she was executed and the Black Rose was dead.

children.title: Sion
children.textContent: Swain obtained Sion from the Black Rose after the organization left Sion in his own tomb for years. Swain now uses him as a living weapon to use against his enemies.

children.title: Vladimir
children.textContent: Vladimir has recently brought himself back into the public eye, becoming a vocal opponent against the Trifarix Council, and this has caught Swain's attention. However, Vladimir's previous public identity has not been completely forgotten, and it is hinted that Swain may know of Vladimir's true nature.

mainContent.textContent:
